message:
refactor(id,storage,label,time): consolidate resolve_issue_id, harden ID parsing, fix blocked cache and transaction API

resolve_issue_id deduplication:
- Extract the duplicated resolve_issue_id() helper from comments.rs, dep.rs,
  graph.rs, label.rs, create.rs, and update.rs into a single canonical
  implementation in cli/commands/mod.rs. All six call sites now use the shared
  version via `super::resolve_issue_id`, eliminating ~90 lines of copy-pasted
  logic. The shared version consistently uses find_matching_ids() for hash
  resolution rather than the older storage.find_ids_by_hash() path.

ID parsing hardening (util/id.rs + validation/mod.rs):
- Add MAX_ID_PREFIX_LEN, MAX_ID_HASH_LEN, and MAX_ID_LENGTH as public
  constants so both the parser and validator share the same limits.
- Enforce total length, prefix length, prefix charset, and hash length
  bounds directly in parse_id(), catching malformed IDs earlier.
- Validate child-path segments are non-empty and purely numeric before
  attempting u32 parse, preventing confusing parse errors.
- Replace the hand-rolled is_valid_id_format() in validation/mod.rs with a
  one-liner delegating to parse_id(), removing ~45 lines of duplicated
  validation logic that could drift from the parser.

with_write_transaction API tightening (storage/sqlite.rs):
- Change signature from `&self, FnMut() -> Result<R>` to
  `&mut self, FnMut(&mut Self) -> Result<R>`. The closure now receives
  `&mut Self` explicitly, replacing the previous pattern of capturing a
  `&SqliteStorage` reference and calling methods on it. This makes the
  borrow checker enforce exclusive access during transactions.
- Add periodic WAL checkpointing after successful commits (every 50
  mutations) to prevent unbounded WAL growth in long-running sessions.
- Rewrite set_config(), set_export_hash(), set_export_hashes(), and
  rebuild_blocked_cache() to use with_write_transaction() instead of
  hand-rolling BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT / ROLLBACK, eliminating ~80 lines
  of duplicated retry-and-rollback boilerplate.

Export hash deduplication (storage/sqlite.rs):
- Add dedupe_export_hash_batch() that collapses duplicate issue IDs in a
  batch, keeping the last value (last-writer-wins). This prevents constraint
  violations when import or sync produces multiple hash entries for the same
  issue in one batch.
- Add test_set_export_hashes_deduplicates_duplicate_issue_ids_last_value_wins
  unit test covering the deduplication.

Blocked cache improvements (storage/sqlite.rs):
- Add load_local_open_child_blockers_impl() which finds parents with open
  children and generates synthetic `:child-open` blocker entries. These are
  applied AFTER standard blocker propagation to avoid a logic cycle where a
  parent blocked only by its children would transitively block those children.
- Exclude template issues (is_template = 1) from blocker calculations and
  epic child counts to prevent templates from inflating blocked/child stats.
- Fix comment: BFS -> DFS for check_cycle() which actually uses depth-first.

Close command: generalize epic-close guard (close.rs):
- The "has open children" skip guard now applies to all parent issues, not
  just epics. The skip message dynamically says "epic" or "parent issue"
  based on the issue type, improving UX for non-epic parent issues.

Label command refactoring (label.rs):
- Introduce PreparedLabelRoute struct and prepare_label_routes() helper that
  resolves all issue IDs upfront before any mutations begin. This means a
  resolution failure for any issue in any route batch will abort the entire
  operation before any labels are added/removed, providing atomic all-or-
  nothing semantics across routed batches.
- Simplify label_add() and label_remove() to accept PreparedLabelRoute
  instead of multiple individual parameters.

Ready command: resolve parent ID (ready.rs):
- Add ID resolution for the --parent flag so users can pass partial/hash IDs
  instead of requiring the full canonical ID.

format_relative_time extraction (util/time.rs + comments.rs):
- Move format_relative_time() from comments.rs to util/time.rs as a public
  function, making it available to other modules. The new version also
  handles future timestamps ("from now" suffix) and uses year = 365 days
  instead of year = 12*30 = 360 days.
- Add test_parse_flexible_relative_negative test for negative relative
  timestamps.

Duration overflow protection (util/time.rs + model/mod.rs):
- Clamp relative time amounts to +/-365,000 days (~1000 years) in both
  parse_flexible_timestamp() and parse_relative_time() to prevent panics
  from chrono::Duration overflow on extreme inputs.
- Clamp the retention-days calculation in Issue::is_expired() to 365,000
  days for the same reason.

Sync module updates (sync/mod.rs):
- Update all with_write_transaction() call sites (finalize_export,
  finalize_incremental_auto_flush, import_from_jsonl) to use the new
  closure signature that receives &mut Self, removing the previous
  `let storage_ref: &SqliteStorage = storage` workaround pattern.

E2E routing tests (tests/e2e_routing.rs):
- Add e2e_routing_label_add_failure_does_not_mutate_earlier_batches: verifies
  that when a routed label add fails on an external batch, no labels are
  applied to the local batch either (atomic rollback semantics).
- Add e2e_routing_update_text_preserves_requested_order_across_routes:
  verifies that text output from a mixed local+external update preserves
  the user's requested issue order.

Import test (tests/jsonl_import_export.rs):
- Add import_deduplicates_export_hash_rebuild_when_multiple_records_target_same_issue:
  verifies that importing multiple JSONL records that resolve to the same
  issue ID produces a single export hash entry (last value wins).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 use crate::storage::SqliteStorage;
+use crate::util::id::{IdResolver, find_matching_ids};
 
 pub mod agents;
 pub mod audit;
@@ -40,6 +41,22 @@ pub mod r#where;
 #[cfg(feature = "self_update")]
 pub mod upgrade;
 
+/// Resolve an issue ID from a potentially partial input.
+pub(super) fn resolve_issue_id(
+    storage: &SqliteStorage,
+    resolver: &IdResolver,
+    all_ids: &[String],
+    input: &str,
+) -> crate::Result<String> {
+    resolver
+        .resolve_fallible(
+            input,
+            |id| storage.id_exists(id),
+            |hash| Ok(find_matching_ids(all_ids, hash)),
+        )
+        .map(|resolved| resolved.id)
+}
+
 pub(super) fn rebuild_blocked_cache_after_partial_mutation(
     storage: &mut SqliteStorage,
     cache_dirty: bool,
